Block.Para: An Act to amend the Public Service Employment Act (priority for appointment for veterans)

Available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca

This enactment amends the Public Service Employment Act to include members of the Canadian Forces who have served in a special duty area for at least 36 months among the groups of persons who have priority for appointment to the public service. This priority also applies to the surviving spouse or surviving common-law partner of an eligible Canadian Forces member, and extends for a period of one year after the member ceases to be a member of the Canadian Forces.
An Act to amend the Public Service Employment Act (priority for appointment for veterans)
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. (1) Section 41 of the Public Service Employment Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Para.change: (1.1) Priority for appointment over all other persons shall be given to

(a) a member of the Canadian Forces who for a cumulative period of at least 36 months has served in a special duty area, for a period of one year after they cease to be a member of the Canadian Forces; or
(b) the survivor of a member of the Canadian Forces described in paragraph (a), for a period of one year after the member ceased to be a member of the Canadian Forces.

Block.Para: (2) Section 41 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6):

Para.change: (7) The following definitions apply in this section.

Block.MarginalNote:
芒聙聹special duty area芒聙聺
脗芦 zone de service sp脙漏cial 脗禄

Para.change: 芒聙聹special duty area芒聙聺 means an area designated as a special duty area under section 69 of the Canadian Forces Members and Veterans Re-establishment and Compensation Act.

Block.MarginalNote:
芒聙聹survivor芒聙聺
脗芦 survivant 脗禄

Para.change: 芒聙聹survivor芒聙聺, in relation to a member of the Canadian Forces, means
(a) a person who was married to the member at the time of the member芒聙聶s death, if there is no person described in paragraph (b); or
(b) a person who was cohabiting with the member in a conjugal relationship for a period of at least one year immediately before the member芒聙聶s death.

Block.Para: 2. Every person who has a priority for appointment under the Public Service Employment Act on the coming into force of this Act continues to have priority for appointment for the period, and in the order, provided for by or under that Act.
Published under authority of the Senate of Canada
